title: teline maderensis var. paivae
intro: Teline maderensis var. paivae, also known as the Madeiran teline, is a plant species within the legume family Fabaceae, native to the island of Madeira. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its alkaloid content, including lupanine and sparteine, which are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with Teline maderensis var. paivae include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and potential use as an antispasmodic agent, making it a valuable plant for herbal remedies. The herbal preparations of Teline maderensis var. paivae typically involve drying and processing the plant material into teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use.
